item.title: Institutional Impartiality
item.description:
Institutional Impartiality

The Institute for Quantitative Social Science seeks to solve society's greatest challenges by helping to create, preserve, and disseminate social science knowledge. We build infrastructure and community so social scientists can work at unprecedented speed, scale, and levels of collaboration, often with high potential for societal impact. We support social science research regardless of the nature of the inquiry, the results it might yield, or the conclusions one might draw from the findings.

item.title: Better Research VI: Data Sharing with Harvard Dataverse
item.description:
Better Research VI: Data Sharing with Harvard Dataverse

calendar_today Date and Time

May 20, 2026
12:30PM - 02:00PM EDT

pin_drop Location

Lamont Library B30 Collaborative Learning Space
Register for Workshop arrow_circle_right

What: Come learn about the Harvard Dataverse for data sharing and preservation, and explore how the repository can support your research project and data sharing needs.

item.title: Dataverse Office Hours
item.description:
Dataverse Office Hours

calendar_today Date and Time

May 20, 2026
12:00PM - 01:00PM EDT
RSVP for Office Hours arrow_circle_right

Weekly virtual office hours are open to Harvard researchers and staff to provide support for Dataverse. Demo of the Dataverse platform will begin promptly at noon.

Open Hours: Wednesdays, 12:00–1:00 PM

item.title: Flourishing Schools Project Virtual Launch
item.description:
Flourishing Schools Project Virtual Launch

calendar_today Date and Time

May 20, 2026
11:00AM - 12:00PM EDT

pin_drop Location

Virtual via Zoom
Register via Zoom arrow_circle_right

Join us for the virtual launch of the Human Flourishing Program at Harvard’s Flourishing Schools Project. This session will introduce a new effort to support school networks in measuring, reflecting on, and strengthening students’ character skills and overall flourishing.

item.title: Research Grants
item.description:
Research Grants

Harvard graduate students currently enrolled in a docotoral program and affiliated with IQSS are eligible to apply for funding to support dissertation research-related costs, including but not limited to:

item.title: Travel Grants
item.description:
Travel Grants

Travel grants are available to IQSS graduate student affiliates currently enrolled in a doctoral program and are meant to support travel to facilitate presentation at a professional conference relevant to their research.

item.title: Our Impact
item.description:
Our Impact

Our Impact

IQSS encourages and allows researchers to think more expansively and take on projects that would otherwise have been impossible. Here are examples of how IQSS drives tangible change in the world.

Investigating Flight Attendants' Safety

In the longest-running longitudinal study of its kind, a study by Dr. Eileen McNeely, executive director of SHINE at IQSS, collected data on uniforms from major US airlines. Laboratory tests found harmful substances on a manufacturer's uniforms, and analysis showed a clear connection between the introduction of these uniforms and reports of debilitating medical issues such as respiratory problems and thyroid disease. These findings provided evidence in a class action lawsuit holding the uniform manufacturer accountable for the resulting health effects.

item.title: Business Influence in Trade, Climate, and Social Policy
item.description:
Business Influence in Trade, Climate, and Social Policy

Faculty lead: Gautam Nair, Assistant Professor of Public Policy, HKS

This project studies lobbying, campaign finance, and other strategies of business influence to understand the conditions under which business influence is beneficial or detrimental for democracy and development.

item.title: Mapping historical military and conflict databases
item.description:
Mapping historical military and conflict databases

Faculty lead: Roya Talibova, Sultan of Oman Assistant Professor of Public Policy

This project explores historical patterns of military engagement, administrative governance, and political violence through the development of a novel, structured database of historical military and administrative records. By systematically scraping and processing datasets from archival websites and digitized government records, we aim to map and analyze key historical events and administrative shifts across various regions and time periods. The research will also involve geocoding spatial data to provide a nuanced, geographic understanding of these patterns, supporting the study of long-term impacts on political stability, militarism, and governance structures. The products of this research will include multiple research papers on historical legacies of warfare, military governance practices, persistence of political violence, and army organization, contributing to studies in historical political economy and state formation.

item.title: For Students
item.description:
For Students

Harvard undergraduates can participate in the research mission of the University by working as paid research assistants to IQSS Faculty Affiliates. Faculty funded by the URS program come from a wide array of schools and disciplines at Harvard. There are two ways you can join our program:

item.title: For Faculty
item.description:
For Faculty

Take advantage of Undergraduate Research Scholars funding to bring in more research support for your project. The URS Program provides grant funding and payroll infrastructure to compensate undergraduates to work as research assistants on approved projects, up to a maximum grant of $1,250 per year.

item.title: Undergraduate Research Scholars
item.description:
Undergraduate Research Scholars

Undergraduate Research Scholars

The IQSS Undergraduate Research Scholars (URS) program is an opportunity for Harvard College students to gain first-hand research experience working with faculty members all across the University.

Program Benefits

More About the Program chevron_right

For Faculty

Get funding to hire Undergraduate Research Scholars as research assistants for your project.

item.title: Extraordinary Claims, Extraordinary Evidence
item.description:
Extraordinary Claims, Extraordinary Evidence

Supporting Harvard Faculty Taking Major Intellectual Risks

The Institute for Quantitative Social Science (IQSS) invites Harvard social science tenure-track faculty to collaborate on research that challenges dominant scientific, political, or cultural assumptions. Through uniquely tailored support — including high-level data expertise, infrastructure, and funding — we help investigators collect the extraordinary evidence required to move the scientific community’s dominant position the farthest distance.

item.title: rapidPeer
item.description:
rapidPeer

rapidPeer is a free service offering double-blind peer reviews of research papers, grants, or preregistration plans in 30 days by field-specific experts at leading institutions

 

Why rapidPeer?

Boost your publication odds

Get a professional-quality review without using a journal submission

item.title: Research Computing at IQSS
item.description:
Research Computing at IQSS

Research Computing at IQSS

IQSS supports social science users on the FAS Research Computing clusters (FASRC).

Get an IQSS Cannon Account arrow_circle_right

Office Hours

Having technical issues using FASRC? General questions? Drop in for FASRC virtual office hours, open to all users and labs.

item.title: Sponsored Research Services
item.description:
Sponsored Research Services

IQSS Research Administration

The Sponsored Research Administration Team at IQSS is dedicated to supporting social scientists' research by providing exceptional services throughout the lifecycle of a sponsored project.

item.title: About WoGPoP
item.description:
About WoGPoP

The Working Group in Political Psychology and Behavior (WOGPOP) is an interdisciplinary forum for the presentation and discussion of current research that uses a psychological and empirical orientation to examine the micro-foundations of citizen and elite behavior. Our topics include but are not limited to identity, emotion, culture, beliefs, preferences (including public opinion and individual preferences), rationality, norms, cognition, group dynamics, ethnic politics, context effects, attribution, information, bargaining and trust. This is a methodologically plural forum open to faculty, graduate students, and other members of the academic community.

item.title: Seminar Series: Alberto Alesina Seminar on Political Economy
item.description:
Seminar Series: Alberto Alesina Seminar on Political Economy

The Faculty of Arts and Sciences and IQSS sponsor this seminar on formal and quantitative political research. The Alberto Alesina Seminar on Political Economy (PE) supports research-related activities that integrate the study of economics and politics, whether by studying economic behavior in the political process or political behavior in the marketplace. In general, positive political economy is concerned with showing how observed differences among institutions affect political and economic outcomes in various social, economic, and political systems and how the institutions themselves change and develop in response to individual and collective beliefs, preferences, and strategies. All interested faculty and students are invited to attend.

item.title: Metrics
item.description:
Metrics

We are committed to tracking the performance and impact of the Extraordinary Claims, Extraordinary Evidence Program through a rigorous and continuous evaluation program. The ultimate, long-term outcome we hope to impact is the empowerment of scholars to effect a major change in the dominant view of their academic field. We will do this by providing the infrastructure and personnel scholars need to collect evidence for their extraordinary claim.

Our evaluation plan focuses on measuring our success in improving the ability of participating scholars to make a case for their extraordinary claim.  We will also track the outcomes of all papers. Participants are asked to complete a short baseline evaluation survey, and will be asked to complete a short final evaluation survey after their paper is published.

item.title: Submissions
item.description:
Submissions

You may follow one of two application tracks, depending on the stage of development of your extraordinary claim:

  1. If you have a clear idea of the nature of your extraordinary claim and what extraordinary evidence you think will be necessary to convince others of it, send a two page PDF proposal to extraordinary@iq.harvard.edu describing the claim, the evidence needed to support it, the likelihood of success, and how different parts of IQSS research services and infrastructure (e.g., people, space, projects, etc.) may be able to help.  Please provide some indicator of what you need. The PDF should include a link to your website (and may optionally include links to any other information you wish to provide).
  2. If development of your extraordinary claim is at a nascent stage and you would like to learn more about how IQSS resources can accelerate this and other research projects, then please talk with us before writing a proposal by emailing extraordinary@iq.harvard.edu. We can provide access to experts in statistics, programming, and project management, who can offer guidance as you develop your research project(s) and ultimately help determine whether it would be useful for you to submit a proposal to the Extraordinary Claims Program.

item.title: Eligibility
item.description:
Eligibility

Eligibility for awards extends to Harvard ladder faculty (individuals or groups) conducting research with a social science component (i.e., any research focused on human beings holistically, rather than on specific anatomical or physiological systems). Awardees must participate in the free IQSS rapidPeer service that speeds the research process through fast, constructive, double-blind peer-review by leading external scholars.

item.title: Selection Process
item.description:
Selection Process

1. Submissions are accepted on the Submissions webpage.

2. We will forward your submission for review to an ad hoc selection committee of Harvard faculty from the relevant field. We aim to complete this step within 1 month.

item.title: What ECEE offers you
item.description:
What ECEE offers you

The small number of awardees chosen will receive an unusually high level of research support from IQSS infrastructure and personnel, all designed to supercharge their research and help them collect the necessary extraordinary evidence for their extraordinary claim.

item.title: Quick Start Guide for Faculty
item.description:
Quick Start Guide for Faculty

Helpful Links:

  • Click here for a more exhaustive version of this article.
  • Zoom For Remote Classes.
  • Zoom, Class scheduling in Canvas.
  • Zoom on Canvas training and more info.
  • Teach Remotely by VPAL and HUIT.

FAQs and Tips: (These will be updated regularly)

  • Please note that there is currently a limit of 300 participants for Zoom meetings or conferences. Please contact HUIT at 617-495-7777 or ithelp@harvard.edu to increase the limit to 500-1000 participants.
  • If you're using a Harvard provided device, there's a good chance Zoom is installed on your computer already. If not, please install it using this link. You will want to click the download button under Zoom Client for Meetings. For further details see this article.
  • Students should make sure that Zoom is installed prior to the class to ensure that they can connect and interact properly and on time.
  • For an individual to unmute and talk, they only need to press and hold the spacebar.
  • It is a good idea to mute all participants and allow them to unmute themselves when and if they need to speak.

item.description:
Item Checkout

We have a variety of items that are available for daily checkout to our patrons by stopping by lab help desk in the Concourse Lab and leaving an active ID from Harvard or MIT (Sorry, no library cards). Items must be returned 30 minutes before the help desk closes, which can vary from day to day due to staffing issues. IDs can only be returned to the ID owner.

item.title: K301 Computer Lab
item.description:
K301 Computer Lab

The K301 Computer Lab is located on the 3rd floor of CGIS Knafel Building (1737 Cambridge Street). To get to K301, take the elevator to the third floor and go to the corner room nearest the circular staircase.

item.description:
Computer Training Room

Our Computer Training Room is for instructor-led events that require students to access a computer and follow the instructor. The room (SB12) is located in the Sub-basement Level of the CGIS Knafel building and is only open during requested reservation times.

item.description:

The Concourse Computer Lab is located in room K024, CGIS Knafel Building (1737 Cambridge Street). To get to this lab, take the stairs or elevator down one level and go to the left through the open door.

After hours building access is 100% handled by CGIS Building Operations, and as such IQSS Computer Lab Services has no ability to grant access or do we have any insight into when (or if) your access will be granted.

item.title: Research Resources
item.description:
Research Resources

Research Resources

IQSS offers access for social scientists to technological resources, and we’re ready to help you learn how to use them so you can focus on your research.

rapidPeer

rapidPeer is an innovative peer pre-review service—similar to peer review for a scholarly journal, but faster, more complete, and constructive. Peer pre-reviewers are asked to read the entire paper and make constructive suggestions, wherever they think of something useful, throughout.

item.title: The IQSS Startup Foundry
item.description:
The IQSS Startup Foundry

The IQSS Startup Foundry

From Insight to Impact

Transforming Social Science Research 

Into World-Changing Companies

The IQSS Startup Foundry helps Harvard faculty transform social science insights into scalable technology companies that solve global challenges.

item.title: R Packages
item.description:
R Packages

For two decades, IQSS has led the development of Open Source analytical tools for researchers. Many of these tools take the form of R packages - modules that provide specialized functionality for the R programming language. These packages are freely available for anyone to use, modify, and contribute to.

item.title: Product Development
item.description:
Product Development

IQSS researches and develops software and systems tools—a unique undertaking in an academic environment. While social science research drives us, we strive to create broad-based, powerful, scalable, extendable products that will be useful in diverse research domains and scholarly environments. We aim to build tools that will enable breakthrough advances in a sweeping range of social problems. IQSS currently is working on the following projects:

item.title: The IQSS Privacy Insights Project
item.description:
The IQSS Privacy Insights Project

Unprecedented quantities of data that could help social scientists understand and ameliorate the challenges of human society are presently locked away inside companies, governments, and other organizations, in part because of worries about privacy violations.  Differential Privacy is a technique that might help change this—a technological solution to the political problem of data sharing. Differential privacy provides mathematical guarantees to protect the privacy of individuals who may be in the data while simultaneously making it possible for social scientists to gain insights into societal level patterns and relationships.  However, many differentially private solutions are statistically invalid—giving biased estimates or no measures of uncertainty.  We aim to fix this.  Just as differential privacy can protect individuals, we are developing statistically valid differentially private systems that will also protect researchers and society so that we can be confident of scholarly conclusions, policy advice, and personal recommendations.

As part of this effort, we are partnering with Microsoft and the Privacy Tools Project (at SEAS) to incubate OpenDP, a set of open source software tools for privacy-protective statistically valid analyses of sensitive personal data; the first end-to-end system we've built together is SmartNoise.  We are also working together to build a broader OpenDP community with stakeholders and contributors from across academia, industry, and government.  Together, we will design, implement, and govern an “OpenDP Commons” that includes a library of differentially private algorithms and other general-purpose tools for use in end-to-end differential privacy systems.

item.title: China Biographical Database Project (CBDB)
item.description:
China Biographical Database Project (CBDB)

Welcome

Introduction

The China Biographical Database is a freely accessible relational database with biographical information about approximately 649,533 individuals as of May 2025, currently mainly from the 7th through 19th centuries. With both online and offline versions, the data is meant to be useful for statistical, social network, and spatial analysis as well as serving as a kind of biographical reference. The image below shows the spatial distribution of a cross dynastic subset of 190,000 people in CBDB by basic affiliations (籍貫).

item.title: Program on Higher Education Research
item.description:
Program on Higher Education Research

 The Institute for Quantitative Social Science (IQSS) has established a new Program on Higher Education Research. IQSS scientific programs focus on areas of inquiry that are of particular interest to faculty directors who enjoy a close working relationship with IQSS administrative, development, and technical staff that provide invaluable infrastructure to researchers.

item.title: The Future of Media Project
item.description:
The Future of Media Project

It is the great civic dilemma of our time. 

The digital age has disrupted the media ecosystem and opened up our access to information, in equal measures. Solving for a robust information ecosystem void of disinformation, hate, and polarization is the problem of our lifetime. Even the other great challenges we face, which are exceptional, from climate change to pandemics to failing trust in democratic institutions will fall prey to distrust and chaos without a strong media ecosystem.

item.description:

item.title: IQSS Startup Foundry
item.description:
IQSS Startup Foundry - Innovation Community

The IQSS Startup Foundry Innovation Community

Social Science Startups: An Untapped Innovation Pipeline

The IQSS Startup Foundry helps Harvard social scientists transform insights from their research into viable startups. We support early-stage software and AI ventures, with commercialization typically taking 6-18 months, depending on factors such as industry complexity, regulation, and initial development stage.

item.title: IQSS Startup Foundry
item.description:
IQSS Startup Foundry - Academic Founders

Bridging Academia and Industry to Solve Global Challenges

Commercialization Help for Academic Founders

The IQSS Startup Foundry supports Harvard’s social scientists from any school in exploring the commercialization of their research into a new commercial venture. Our sweet spot is very early-stage concepts born from validated Harvard research.

item.description:

Undergraduate Research Scholars

Program Benefits

For Faculty

Get funding to hire Undergraduate Research Scholars as research assistants for your project.

Research Grants

Harvard graduate students currently enrolled in a docotoral program and affiliated with IQSS are eligible to apply for funding to support dissertation research-related costs, including but not limited to:

item.title: Travel Grants

Travel Grants

Travel grants are available to IQSS graduate student affiliates currently enrolled in a doctoral program and are meant to support travel to facilitate presentation at a professional conference relevant to their research.

item.title: Roadmap: The Dataverse Project
item.description:
Roadmap: The Dataverse Project

Strategic Goals

The Strategic Goals of the Dataverse Project are to:

  • Grow the Dataverse community
  • Empower the open source community to explore and implement new Dataverse applications, tools, and services
  • Develop the capability to handle sensitive data and big data
  • Expand data and metadata features for existing and new disciplines
  • Expand archival and preservation features
  • Increase interoperability through the implementation of standards
  • Increase contributions from the open source development community
  • Improve the Dataverse user experience

Roadmap

The Dataverse UI is being redesigned as a single-page application (SPA) in which internal and external Dataverse services will be delivered solely through improved APIs. This change will improve UI responsiveness and empower the Dataverse community to develop their own UIs and applications using extended Dataverse API endpoints.

item.title: Roadmap: Sid & IQSS Research Computing
item.description:
Roadmap: Sid & IQSS Research Computing

Sid Verba was a beloved professor who unified disparate groups and made Harvard better.  Sid is our branding of the Open OnDemand software to unify and enhance the research computing user experience at Harvard.

item.title: Roadmap: Data Science Services
item.description:
Roadmap: Data Science Services

Our winning aspiration

To be the premier Harvard research facilitation organization, enabling clients to produce high-quality research at optimal speed.

Our playing field

We’ll continue to support:

  • Universities: All Harvard and MIT departments
  • Roles: Anyone doing publication research
  • Research Outputs: Any research artifacts that involve statistics and/or programming
  • Services: Consulting; collaboration; tools

Our recipe for success

  • Give researchers a competitive edge: Reduce overall time to publication by helping users quickly overcome statistics- and programming-related obstacles. Help users produce solid statistical analysis and high-quality research outputs & packages by providing timely, first-rate guidance and feedback.
  • Support all stages of the research lifecycle: Support users from early research design to post-paper submission

Our approach to scaling

  • Targeted scaling: Analytics-based targeted marketing efforts and departmental reach-out activities, towards the goals of keeping client engagement across all Harvard departments and continuing word-of-mouth growth. 

Our Roadmap

View full size

item.title: Roadmap: rapidPeer
item.description:
Roadmap: rapidPeer

Our winning aspiration

Our aim is to set a new standard for the research journey across Harvard and the world by speeding up the delivery of quality, trustworthy research.

item.title: Become an Affiliate
item.description:
Become an Affiliate

IQSS welcomes requests from scholars who participate in the life of the Institute to become affiliates. Opportunities to participate in the life of the Institute include attending seminars, giving talks to the IQSS commmunity, working with IQSS graduate student affiliates, co-authoring or otherwise collaborating with IQSS affiliates, serving on committees, reviewing scholarly grant applications, attenting events, and sharing in a pot of coffee in the IQSS "Quantina" common space. Below is a list of affiliations and requirements. Affiliation also allows graduate students and Harvard Faculty to apply for funding or access IQSS-based services, such as Sponsored Research Services. If you have further questions, please contact affiliation@iq.harvard.edu.

item.title: Support Our Work
item.description:
Support Our Work

Giving to the Institute for Quantitative Social Science at Harvard University

You can help IQSS break new ground in social science research.

While people at IQSS are the world's leading experts in their field, they are super-friendly and welcoming at the same time. Feels like an ideal combination.

item.title: rapidPeer
item.description:
rapidPeer

rapidPeer is a free service offering double-blind peer reviews of research papers, grants, or preregistration plans in 30 days by field-specific experts at leading institutions

 

Why rapidPeer?

Boost your publication odds

Get a professional-quality review without using a journal submission

item.title: Mindich Encounters with Authors Conference Series
item.description:
Mindich Encounters with Authors Conference Series

The annual Eric M. Mindich Encounters with Authors Conference is an opportunity for one or more authors to present a major work still in progress.

item.title: Dissertation Awards
item.description:
Dissertation Awards

The Henry A. Murray Research Archive is pleased to offer the Jeanne Humphrey Block Dissertation Award to a qualified PhD candidate in the social sciences each spring.

item.title: Funding
item.description:
Funding

IQSS supports the research efforts of its affiliated faculty, graduate students and undergraduates via a variety of funding opportunities designed to move the frontiers of social science knowledge. Funding opportunities are available in the categories listed below.
